description_long: This is a three-story apartment building with a rectangular footprint and a hipped roof covered in gray shingles. The exterior is clad in light gray horizontal siding, giving it a clean and uniform appearance. Windows are arranged in a regular grid pattern, with each floor featuring a mix of single and double-pane blue-framed windows, some with white trim. The front facade includes a recessed main entrance with a small awning above the door, providing shelter and visual emphasis. There are no visible balconies, and the building lacks ornate architectural details, favoring a simple, functional design. The structure appears to be constructed primarily from wood framing and concrete, typical of mid- to late-20th-century residential architecture.
description: A three-story, light gray apartment building with a hipped roof, blue-framed windows, and a recessed main entrance. The design is simple and functional, with regularly spaced windows and no balconies.
description_view_0: This view shows the narrow side of the building with three floors, several blue-framed windows, and a double-door entrance at the bottom.
description_view_1: This view displays the long side of the building, featuring a symmetrical arrangement of windows across all three floors and no visible entrance.
description_view_2: This view presents the opposite narrow side, with only a few small windows and no doors or other features.
description_view_3: This is the front view, highlighting the main entrance with an awning, a vertical row of windows above the door, and a balanced window layout on both sides.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in a regular, grid-like pattern with consistent spacing and alignment across all floors, creating a symmetrical and orderly appearance.
